title: Set First Column to Date/calendar
excerpt: I can’t seem to be able to do this. I need tyo see a timeline in the first column. TIA

posts.author: VPPR (Barry Koblenz)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t seem to be able to do this. I need tyo see a timeline in the first column. TIA

posts.author: kindertech (KinderTech)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey the @VPPR, are you able to give some clarification on what you need help with. If you could expound on why the timeline would need to be first that may help too.

Do you mean the item’s column or the column immediately following the items column?

image565×206 7.74 KB

If you want to see a timeline in the first column, where the item name appears you could create an automation that creates the new item with it’s name as the timeline you want and then describe the information after.

Alternatively, you could add a Gantt view to your board. Then you will be able to view everything on your board from the “timeline perspective”

If you need any more in depth help, set up a free meeting with me below and we can chat. Calendly - Nathan Kind

All the best,
Nathan Kind (nathan@kinder-technologies.com)
Lead Software Consultant - Kinder Tech

posts.author: VPPR (Barry Koblenz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Nathan,

Thank you for responding. Having the timeline in the first column is to help see at a glance which projects are in progress or upcoming. In the example below, ‘Timeline 1’ would be the first column and ‘Task’ the second column.

If you want to see a timeline in the first column, where the item name appears you could create an automation that creates the new item with it’s name as the timeline you want and then describe the information after.

Could you clarify how I would do this?

Thanks,

– Barry

Barry Koblenz, SR3
Vice President of Public Relations
